tourOverview.name: Fuji Soul Tours – Mt. Fuji Cultural Private Tour
tourOverview.tagline: Turn your Shinkansen travel day into a Mt. Fuji cultural highlight.
tourOverview.description: Stop at Shin-Fuji Station on your bullet train journey between Tokyo and Kyoto/Osaka, and explore Mt. Fuji with a local-born guide — away from the tourist hubs.
tourOverview.meetingPoint: Your guide will wait right at the gate exit with a 'Fuji Soul Tours' sign.
tourOverview.availableHours: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM (flexible based on your train schedule)
tourOverview.recommendation: The earlier the better — morning hours tend to offer clearer views of Mt. Fuji.

guide.name: Your guide
guide.background: Local-born, Fuji-native, bilingual English speaker, 10+ years local expertise.
guide.philosophy: Show you the Mt. Fuji that locals know and love, not just what's in guidebooks and viral reels. No tourist traps, only places we'd take our own family.

pricing.promo: LIMITED-TIME 15% OFF SALE through June 16, 2026 (campaign promo15). The prices below are the REGULAR rate; during the sale the actual charge is 15% lower. Sale prices: 1 guest ¥58,650 (~$424 USD), 2 guests ¥29,325 per person (~$213 USD), 3–5 guests ¥21,522 per person (~$153 USD, best rate). When quoting prices, mention this 15% discount and that it ends June 16, 2026.
pricing.largeGroup: For groups of 6–10, additional ¥100,000 (non-refundable, paid in advance) on top of the standard per-person rate for 5 guests.
pricing.extensionRate: $100 USD (¥16,500 JPY) per additional hour beyond the standard 4 hours.
pricing.extras: Activities and food at stops are paid separately on-site (not included in tour price).

whatsIncluded: Private tour for your group only (max 5 guests)
Premium air-conditioned van with luggage storage
Hidden photo spots — curated viewpoints locals love

bookingCutoff.minimumAdvance: Bookings require at least 2 days advance notice. Same-day and next-day (tomorrow) bookings are not accepted by the booking system.
bookingCutoff.earliestBookable: The earliest you can book is 2 days from today (the day after tomorrow), subject to availability.
bookingCutoff.note: Even if you can see a date on the calendar, availability is limited — check the booking widget for real-time availability.

cancellationPolicy.24hPlus: Full refund or free date change
cancellationPolicy.howToCancel: Use the 'Manage my booking' button in your booking confirmation email.
cancellationPolicy.dateChange: Contact us or use the date change form on the website.

faq.q: Will this disrupt my travel schedule?
faq.a: No. The 4-hour tour with flexible timing means you can still reach Tokyo, Kyoto, or Osaka the same day. We plan around your Shinkansen schedule.

faq.q: Can I extend the tour duration?
faq.a: Yes! Extended tour time is available at $100 USD (¥16,500 JPY) per additional hour. If you have many activities on your wish list that won't fit in 4 hours, your guide will create an itinerary showing the extra time needed, and you can decide to either trim activities or pay for the extension.

faq.q: What about my luggage?
faq.a: Large suitcases welcome! Secure storage in our van throughout the tour. For groups of 4–5 with large bags, we'll use station or tourist office lockers nearby (¥300–800 per piece, cash only).

faq.q: Where do we meet?
faq.a: Shin-Fuji Station exit. Your guide will wait right at the gate exit with a 'Fuji Soul Tours' sign.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad or my train is delayed?
faq.a: We have backup plans with alternative cultural experiences and offer flexible rescheduling. Real-time communication via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Can I join from Osaka/Kyoto direction?
faq.a: Yes. Shin-Fuji is perfectly located between Osaka/Kyoto and Tokyo, making it ideal for a stopover on your return journey too.

faq.q: Can I do a Tokyo → Shin-Fuji → Tokyo round trip?
faq.a: Yes. Shin-Fuji is only 1 hour from Tokyo, so a round trip in one day is easy and comfortable.

faq.q: What time does the tour start?
faq.a: Flexible — we adjust the start time based on your train schedule. Available 8:00–17:00. The earlier the better for clearer Mt. Fuji views.

faq.q: Can children under 6 or infants join the tour?
faq.a: Japanese law requires a child safety seat for children aged 5 and under. We do not normally carry child seats, so we are usually unable to accommodate young children. However, depending on the date and number of guests, we may be able to rent a child seat for $50 USD. Infants count as a passenger but do not need a separate booking — book for the number of adults only. Please contact us with your date, group size, and child's age so we can check availability.

faq.q: Can I book for more than 5 guests?
faq.a: Our standard tour accommodates up to 5 guests. For groups of 6–10, we may be able to arrange a larger van — this requires an additional ¥100,000 (non-refundable, paid in advance) on top of the standard per-person rate for 5 guests. For groups larger than 10, we coordinate multiple vehicles. Please contact us for a custom quote.

faq.q: What if someone in my group has mobility difficulties?
faq.a: We can adjust the destinations based on your mobility level — some stops require less walking than others. Your guide will tailor the route accordingly. If you use a walker, we can store it in the trunk, though the maximum group size is reduced to 3 guests. Unfortunately, we are unable to accommodate wheelchairs as our vehicle is not equipped for them. Please contact us in advance to plan the best itinerary.

faq.q: When can you see Mt. Fuji?
faq.a: Mt. Fuji is visible approximately 40% of the year. Best months: Nov–Feb (60–73% visibility). Summer: 13–17% (rainy/humid). Even when Mt. Fuji hides in the clouds, cultural experiences make this tour rewarding. Your guide adjusts the route to maximize your experience regardless of visibility.

faq.q: What happens on a clear day vs a cloudy day?
faq.a: On clear days with Mt. Fuji visible, we prioritize scenic viewpoints — lakeside spots, highland views, and getting close to the mountain. On cloudy days when Fuji is hidden, we focus more on cultural experiences — Shiraito Falls (UNESCO), the principal shrine, local yakisoba lunch, and a traditional tea house for green tea with Japanese sweets. Either way, your guide tailors the route to give you the best experience.

faq.q: Are entry fees and food included in the tour price?
faq.a: No. The tour price covers private transportation, your guide, and bottled water. All entry fees, food, drinks, and activity costs at each stop are paid separately on-site. Each destination card on our website shows the estimated cost so you can plan ahead.

faq.q: How do I buy Shinkansen tickets to Shin-Fuji?
faq.a: Use SmartEX (https://smart-ex.jp/en/index.php) — the only official booking site for Shinkansen, with the best change and cancellation terms. All other booking sites are third-party. If the SmartEX mobile app cannot be downloaded on your device, you can book through the web browser instead. You can also buy tickets at the station, but booking ahead guarantees your seat. Remember: only the Kodama train stops at Shin-Fuji — not Nozomi or Hikari.

faq.q: Why can't I find Shin-Fuji Station on Klook? / I can't find Shin-Fuji on Klook
faq.a: Klook and other third-party booking platforms often don't list Shin-Fuji Station because only the Kodama Shinkansen stops there (not Nozomi or Hikari). This is a Klook limitation, not a problem with the station itself — Shin-Fuji is a fully operational JR Tokaido Shinkansen station. To book Shinkansen tickets to Shin-Fuji, use SmartEX (https://smart-ex.jp/en/index.php), JR's official Shinkansen booking website. SmartEX lists all stations including Shin-Fuji and offers the best change/cancellation terms. If the SmartEX mobile app is unavailable in your country's app store, you can book through the web browser version instead. You can also buy tickets directly at any JR station in Japan.

faq.q: How do I get to Shin-Fuji from central Tokyo?
faq.a: From central Tokyo (e.g., Shinagawa Station), take the Kodama Shinkansen. It's about 1 hour. From most Tokyo hotels, take a taxi or train to Tokyo Station or Shinagawa Station, then hop on the Kodama bullet train.

faq.q: Can you share exact locations of the photo spots?
faq.a: We keep our curated spots private — it's the core value of our 'crowd-free' experience. Exact locations are shared only with booked guests so these hidden gems stay uncrowded. We can share general descriptions of what you'll see, but not specific addresses or map pins.

faq.q: Can I customize the tour stops?
faq.a: Yes! You can add or swap destinations and experiences based on your interests and timing. After booking, share your preferences and we'll tailor the itinerary for you.

faq.q: Can you pick us up from our hotel in Tokyo?
faq.a: Unfortunately no. The tour starts and ends at Shin-Fuji Station only. It's much faster to take the bullet train (about 1 hour from Tokyo) than to drive (2.5+ hours by car). We recommend taking the Kodama Shinkansen from Tokyo or Shinagawa Station.

faq.q: What happens after I book?
faq.a: After booking, your guide will reach out via WhatsApp or email to: 1) Confirm your train schedule, 2) Ask about your activity preferences, 3) Create a personalized itinerary based on your interests and timing. The itinerary can be revised until tour day.

faq.q: Where can I read reviews?
faq.a: You can find reviews on TripAdvisor and Viator. We're rated 5.0 stars. Search for 'Fuji Soul Tours' on either platform.

importantNotes.cash: Bring Japanese Yen cash. Some spots are cash-only — lockers, kimono rental, shrine offerings, certain cafés. Most restaurants and shops also accept cards.
importantNotes.train: Take the Kodama (こだま) train. Only Kodama stops at Shin-Fuji Station — not Nozomi or Hikari. Covered by JR Pass and usually less crowded.
importantNotes.ticketBooking: Book Shinkansen tickets via SmartEX (https://smart-ex.jp/en/index.php) — the only official Shinkansen booking site with the best change/cancellation terms. All other sites are third-party. If the SmartEX mobile app cannot be downloaded on your device, use the web browser instead. You can also buy at the station, but advance booking guarantees your seat.
importantNotes.seats: Best Shinkansen seats for Mt. Fuji views: From Tokyo → sit in seat E (right-side window). From Kyoto/Osaka → sit in seat A (left-side window).
importantNotes.travelTimes: Shin-Fuji Station is about 1 hour from Tokyo/Shinagawa, about 2 hours from Kyoto, and about 2.5 hours from Osaka.
importantNotes.locationPrivacy: Exact photo spot locations are kept private and shared only with booked guests to preserve the crowd-free experience.

instagramFAQ.q: Can I bring an infant on the tour?
instagramFAQ.a: Depending on date and group size, yes. Child seat rental is $50 USD. Infants don't need a separate booking — book for adults only. Contact us with details.

instagramFAQ.q: Where do I leave my luggage if moving to the next city after the tour?
instagramFAQ.a: Large suitcases are stored securely in our van during the tour. For groups of 4-5 with large bags, we use station or tourist office lockers nearby (¥300-800 per piece, cash only).

instagramFAQ.q: Are entry fees and food included?
instagramFAQ.a: No, any fees incurred onsite are not included in the tour price. Each destination card on the website describes the expected costs.

instagramFAQ.q: What's different on clear vs cloudy days?
instagramFAQ.a: Clear days: scenic viewpoints, lake, farm, mountain views. Cloudy days: Shiraito Falls, principal shrine, yakisoba lunch, tea house for green tea with Japanese sweets.

instagramFAQ.q: How do I buy bullet train tickets?
instagramFAQ.a: Use SmartEX (https://smart-ex.jp/en/index.php) — the only official Shinkansen booking site with the best change/cancellation terms. All other sites are third-party. If the mobile app cannot be downloaded, use the web browser instead. You can also buy at the station.

instagramFAQ.q: Can you share exact photo spot locations?
instagramFAQ.a: No, exact locations are kept private for booked guests only to preserve the crowd-free experience.

instagramFAQ.q: How do I get from Tokyo to Shin-Fuji?
instagramFAQ.a: Take a taxi to Shinagawa Station (~13 min from central Tokyo), then ride the Kodama Shinkansen for about 1 hour to Shin-Fuji.

whatsappFAQ.q: Can you pick us up from Tokyo?
whatsappFAQ.a: No, the tour starts and ends at Shin-Fuji Station only. Bullet train takes 1 hour vs 2.5+ hours by car from Tokyo.

whatsappFAQ.q: How much to extend the tour?
whatsappFAQ.a: $100 USD (¥16,500 JPY) per additional hour beyond the standard 4 hours.
Guide reaches out via WhatsApp/email to confirm train schedule, ask about preferences, and create a personalized itinerary. The itinerary can be revised until tour day.

whatsappFAQ.q: Can I plan Tokyo to Kyoto via Mt. Fuji as one trip?
whatsappFAQ.a: Yes, that's exactly what the tour is designed for. Stop at Shin-Fuji on your Shinkansen journey, do the 4h tour, then continue to your next destination.
